---
title: "Assignment 10A"
author: "Leon Lin"
date: "2026-04-16"
output: html_document
---

```{r}

library(tidytext)
library(janeaustenr)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(wordcloud)
library(reshape2)
```

```{r}

get_sentiments("afinn") |> head(10)
get_sentiments("bing")  |> head(10)
get_sentiments("nrc")   |> head(10)
```

```{r}
tidy_books <- austen_books() |>
  group_by(book) |>
  mutate(
    linenumber = row_number(),
    chapter    = cumsum(str_detect(
      text,
      regex("^chapter [\\divxlc]", ignore_case = TRUE)
    ))
  ) |>
  ungroup() |>
  unnest_tokens(word, text)

tidy_books
```


```{r}
nrc_joy <- get_sentiments("nrc") |> filter(sentiment == "joy")

tidy_books |>
  filter(book == "Emma") |>
  inner_join(nrc_joy, by = "word") |>
  count(word, sort = TRUE) |>
  slice_head(n = 15)
```


```{r}
jane_austen_sentiment <- tidy_books |>
  inner_join(get_sentiments("bing"), by = "word") |>
  count(book, index = linenumber %/% 80, sentiment) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = sentiment, values_from = n, values_fill = 0) |>
  mutate(sentiment = positive - negative)

ggplot(jane_austen_sentiment, aes(index, sentiment, fill = book)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~book, ncol = 2, scales = "free_x") +
  labs(
    title = "Sentiment Through Jane Austen's Novels",
    x     = "Narrative Index ",
    y     = "Neet Sentiment"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```



```{r}

pride_prejudice <- tidy_books |> filter(book == "Pride & Prejudice")

afinn <- pride_prejudice |>
  inner_join(get_sentiments("afinn"), by = "word") |>
  group_by(index = linenumber %/% 80) |>
  summarise(sentiment = sum(value)) |>
  mutate(method = "AFINN")

bing_and_nrc <- bind_rows(
  pride_prejudice |>
    inner_join(get_sentiments("bing"), by = "word") |>
    mutate(method = "Bing et al."),
  pride_prejudice |>
    inner_join(
      get_sentiments("nrc") |> filter(sentiment %in% c("positive", "negative")),
      by = "word"
    ) |>
    mutate(method = "NRC")
) |>
  count(method, index = linenumber %/% 80, sentiment) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = sentiment, values_from = n, values_fill = 0) |>
  mutate(sentiment = positive - negative)

bind_rows(afinn, bing_and_nrc) |>
  ggplot(aes(index, sentiment, fill = method)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~method, ncol = 1, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "Three Sentiment Lexicons: Pride and Prejudice",
    x     = "Narrative Index (80-line chunks)",
    y     = "Net Sentiment"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```


```{r}
bing_word_counts <- tidy_books |>
  inner_join(get_sentiments("bing"), by = "word") |>
  count(word, sentiment, sort = TRUE) |>
  ungroup()

bing_word_counts |>
  group_by(sentiment) |>
  slice_max(n, n = 10) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(word = reorder(word, n)) |>
  ggplot(aes(n, word, fill = sentiment)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~sentiment, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "Words Contributing Most to Sentiment in Austen's Novels",
    x     = "Count",
    y     = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```


```{r}
custom_stop_words <- bind_rows(
  tibble(word = c("miss"), lexicon = c("custom")),
  stop_words
)
custom_stop_words |> filter(lexicon == "custom")
```


```{r}

tidy_books |>
  inner_join(get_sentiments("bing"), by = "word") |>
  count(word, sentiment, sort = TRUE) |>
  acast(word ~ sentiment, value.var = "n", fill = 0) |>
  comparison.cloud(colors = c("firebrick", "steelblue"), max.words = 100)
```



```{r}
bingnegative <- get_sentiments("bing") |> filter(sentiment == "negative")

wordcounts <- tidy_books |>
  group_by(book, chapter) |>
  summarize(words = n(), .groups = "drop")

tidy_books |>
  semi_join(bingnegative, by = "word") |>
  group_by(book, chapter) |>
  summarize(negativewords = n(), .groups = "drop") |>
  left_join(wordcounts, by = c("book", "chapter")) |>
  mutate(ratio = negativewords / words) |>
  filter(chapter != 0) |>
  slice_max(ratio, n = 1, by = book) |>
  arrange(book)
```

---

We replace Jane Austen's novels with four Charles Dickens novels obtained
from Project Gutenberg using the `gutenbergr` package:

| Title | Gutenberg ID |
|---|---|
| A Tale of Two Cities | 98 |
| Great Expectations | 1400 |
| Oliver Twist | 730 |
| David Copperfield | 766 |

```{r}
library(gutenbergr)

dickens_ids <- c(98, 1400, 730, 766)

dickens_raw <- gutenberg_download(
  dickens_ids,
  meta_fields = "title",
  mirror      = "https://gutenberg.pglaf.org/"
)

# Recode short titles for cleaner plots
dickens_raw <- dickens_raw |>
  mutate(title = case_when(
    gutenberg_id == 98   ~ "A Tale of Two Cities",
    gutenberg_id == 1400 ~ "Great Expectations",
    gutenberg_id == 730  ~ "Oliver Twist",
    gutenberg_id == 766  ~ "David Copperfield"
  ))

head(dickens_raw)
```

```{r}
tidy_dickens <- dickens_raw |>
  group_by(title) |>
  mutate(
    linenumber = row_number(),
    chapter    = cumsum(str_detect(
      text,
      regex("^chapter [\\divxlc]", ignore_case = TRUE)
    ))
  ) |>
  ungroup() |>
  unnest_tokens(word, text)

tidy_dickens
```


```{r}
dickens_sentiment_bing <- tidy_dickens |>
  inner_join(get_sentiments("bing"), by = "word") |>
  count(title, index = linenumber %/% 80, sentiment) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = sentiment, values_from = n, values_fill = 0) |>
  mutate(sentiment = positive - negative)

ggplot(dickens_sentiment_bing, aes(index, sentiment, fill = title)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~title, ncol = 2, scales = "free_x") +
  labs(
    title = "Sentiment Through Charles Dickens' Novels (Bing)",
    x     = "Narrative Index",
    y     = "Net Sentiment"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```


```{r}
tidy_dickens |>
  filter(title == "Great Expectations") |>
  inner_join(nrc_joy, by = "word") |>
  count(word, sort = TRUE) |>
  slice_head(n = 15)
```


```{r}

tidy_dickens |>
  inner_join(get_sentiments("bing"), by = "word") |>
  count(word, sentiment, sort = TRUE) |>
  group_by(sentiment) |>
  slice_max(n, n = 10) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(word = reorder(word, n)) |>
  ggplot(aes(n, word, fill = sentiment)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~sentiment, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "Words Contributing Most to Sentiment in Dickens' Novels",
    x     = "Count",
    y     = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```


```{r}
library(syuzhet)

syuzhet_lex <- get_sentiment_dictionary(dictionary = "syuzhet") |>
  as_tibble()

# Inspect actual column names (run once to confirm)
glimpse(syuzhet_lex)

# Then rename based on what glimpse shows — typically "word" and "value"
syuzhet_lex <- syuzhet_lex |>
  rename(syuzhet_score = value)   # "word" column usually already named correctly
```



```{r}
tale <- tidy_dickens |> filter(title == "A Tale of Two Cities")

afinn_tale <- tale |>
  inner_join(get_sentiments("afinn"), by = "word") |>
  group_by(index = linenumber %/% 80) |>
  summarise(sentiment = sum(value)) |>
  mutate(method = "AFINN")

syuzhet_tale <- tale |>
  inner_join(syuzhet_lex, by = "word") |>
  group_by(index = linenumber %/% 80) |>
  summarise(sentiment = sum(syuzhet_score)) |>
  mutate(method = "Syuzhet (Jockers)")

bind_rows(afinn_tale, syuzhet_tale) |>
  ggplot(aes(index, sentiment, fill = method)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~method, ncol = 1, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "AFINN vs. Syuzhet: A Tale of Two Cities",
    x     = "Narrative Index ",
    y     = "Net Sentiment Score"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```



```{r}
dickens_syuzhet <- tidy_dickens |>
  inner_join(syuzhet_lex, by = "word") |>
  group_by(title, index = linenumber %/% 80) |>
  summarise(sentiment = sum(syuzhet_score), .groups = "drop")

ggplot(dickens_syuzhet, aes(index, sentiment, fill = title)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~title, ncol = 2, scales = "free_x") +
  labs(
    title = "Syuzhet Sentiment Through Charles Dickens' Novels",
    x     = "Narrative Index",
    y     = "Net Syuzhet Score"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```


```{r}
syuzhet_words <- tidy_dickens |>
  inner_join(syuzhet_lex, by = "word") |>
  distinct(word, syuzhet_score)

afinn_words <- tidy_dickens |>
  inner_join(get_sentiments("afinn"), by = "word") |>
  distinct(word)

syuzhet_only <- syuzhet_words |>
  anti_join(afinn_words, by = "word") |>
  arrange(desc(abs(syuzhet_score)))

cat("Words in Syuzhet but not AFINN (Dickens corpus):", nrow(syuzhet_only), "\n")

syuzhet_only |> slice_head(n = 20)
```
